updated_conclusion: The scheduler/state calls inside worker publish callback 0x14066F3F0 are no longer opaque. The callback first writes wrapper+0x18 into a thread-local label/string slot through 0x1407A4620, then records a bounded scheduler/event interval through 0x1407A6630 before pumping the payload. After the pump exits, 0x1407A6990 takes a global scheduler lock and reconciles thread-local/global counters, and 0x1407B6B58 drains matching queued completion records after the active-entry vector publish.

tooling_milestone.capability: clean native instruction and call-window exports for scheduler helpers
tooling_milestone.change: Extended pe-instructions for byte shifts, TEST accumulator immediates, byte-sized ModRM MOV/CMP, TEST r/m8,r8, CMOVcc, and visible segment overrides such as GS:[0x58]. Also changed pe-function-calls to advance by decoded instruction lengths so embedded E8 bytes inside non-call instructions are not reported as false direct calls.
tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: The scheduler/state helper windows now decode with zero unknown instructions and pe-function-calls no longer misidentifies the E8 byte in MOV EBP,EAX as a callsite.

observed_behavior: Loads the current thread-local storage base through GS:[0x58] and checks byte TLS+0x14, calling 0x1407B89FC when the TLS state is not initialized.
Uses TLS+0xD30 as a small-string-like slot. If the current pointer at TLS+0xD30 equals the incoming RCX value, the helper returns without mutation.

When changing the slot, inspects flag byte +0x17 to decide whether the old storage is inline or heap-backed, clears the first byte of the old storage, scans the incoming C string for length, then tail-jumps to 0x14079C590 with RDX set to the incoming string/pointer.
In the worker callback, the incoming value is wrapper+0x18, making this a thread-local active worker label/context setter rather than a payload object mutator.
thread_local_label_setter_0x1407a4620.confidence: medium-high for TLS string-slot behavior; medium for exact user-facing label name

observed_behavior: Ensures TLS initialization through the same GS:[0x58] +0x14 guard and 0x1407B89FC fallback.
Builds or refreshes a thread-local scheduler record rooted at TLS+0x2450 by calling 0x1407A71A0 with RDX=the original RCX value, R8D=0x100, R9D=1, and stack argument 0x10.
Copies TLS+0x2450+0x30 into +0xB0, stores +0x30 + (original RCX << 8) into +0xB8, writes +0xC0=0x100, +0xC8=0x10, and +0xD0=0.
Increments a per-thread counter at TLS+0x130.

If 0x1407A4220 reports true, mirrors a related global/static record at 0x140E79630 through another 0x1407A71A0 call with R9D=5, copies a template from 0x140BAC600 into 0x140E796F0, sets 0x140E796FC to 4, and increments global counter 0x140E57CB0.
scheduler_interval_marker_0x1407a6630.confidence: medium-high for field writes and counters; medium for exact event semantics

observed_behavior: Locks global object 0x140E86E30 through 0x1407B5200 and reports lock failure through 0x1407B59CC.
Uses TLS+0x130 as a count, iterating that many times and ensuring the TLS byte at +0x14 is initialized through 0x1407B89FC when needed.
Caches the result of indirect call qword ptr [0x140802388] into TLS+0xFC58 when that TLS slot is zero.
Compares global 0x140E79620 against TLS+0xFC58. When they match and global 0x140E57CB0 is non-zero, it spins across reads of 0x140E57CB0 until a local index catches the current global value.
scheduler_signal_gate_0x1407a6990.confidence: medium for synchronization shape; low-medium for the precise meaning of 0x140E79620 and TLS+0xFC58

observed_behavior: Uses global block/list root 0x140C55B00, whose nearby setup helpers also reference 0x1407B62EC, 0x1407B636C, 0x1407B6C04, and 0x1407B6CC8.
Calls 0x1407B5A84 and 0x1407B63DC up front; the first return value in EBP is used as a match key for queued entries.
Walks linked blocks via +0x328. Each block tracks an active count at +0x320 and up to 0x14 entries starting at +0x10, each 0x28 bytes wide.

For a populated entry whose key at entry-0x8 matches EBP, optionally writes 1 through entry+0x10, unlocks or releases entry+0x0 through 0x1407B5260, calls 0x1407B5BF8 on entry+0x8, clears entry+0x0, and decrements the block count.
Tail-jumps to 0x1407B63E8 after scanning the list.
completion_queue_drain_0x1407b6b58.confidence: medium-high for queue/list mechanics; medium for exact notification semantics

relationship_to_worker_publish_callback_.role: Set the current thread-local worker/context label from wrapper+0x18 before the payload state machine starts.
Record a scheduler interval/event marker using constants 0x10 and 0 before payload pumping.
Reconcile scheduler/global counters after the payload vtable pump exits and before active-entry publication.
Drain completion/notification records after the active-entry vector append and parent+0x90 unlock.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-callback-wrapper-and-native-instr: Refines the earlier scheduler placeholders in 0x14066F3F0 into concrete TLS/global scheduler helper roles.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-active-entry-vector-lifecycle: Places the scheduler gate before active-entry vector append and the completion queue drain after publish/unlock, clarifying the ordering around parent+0xE0 publication.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.reverser-tooling: Adds native decoding coverage for common MSVC byte/CMOV/TLS instruction forms and makes function-call scanning instruction-aware.

next_targets: Trace 0x1407A71A0 to name the scheduler record builder used by 0x1407A6630.
Trace 0x1407A4220 to explain when the global/static mirror path at 0x140E79630 activates.
Trace 0x1407B5A84, 0x1407B63DC, 0x1407B5BF8, and 0x1407B63E8 enough to name the 0x140C55B00 completion queue family.
Continue provider/object constructor tracing at 0x14066D010, 0x1400D24B0, and 0x1400D1990 to connect construction-time labels to these scheduler/helper paths.
